steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage concludes that Stobbe had better-explained methodology than Streicher-Porte. The passage defines "better explained methodology" through traceability scores — how clearly procedures are described. To weaken the conclusion, a choice must show Stobbe's traceability was not higher.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Stobbe had lower traceability than Streicher-Porte: directly contradicts the conclusion — if Stobbe scored lower on the metric that defines "better explained," the conclusion fails. ✓
  • Choice B — Stobbe had higher traceability: supports the conclusion rather than weakening it. ✗
  • Choice C — both had equal traceability: neutral at best; doesn't clearly undermine the claim. ✗
  • Choice D — Stobbe scored higher on a different dimension: measures something other than the passage's stated metric. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
